description: SPV Ireland Ltd — Ireland — Interest — UNCERTAIN (EU Directive candidate vs. thin substance)
legal_notes: Ireland treaty: MLI PPT applies. EU I&R Directive potentially applies (EU member; associated company test). However, SPV has minimal substance — one director, no employees. BO condition (ii): SPV has contractual obligation to pass interest to a non-EU parent, which may block both Directive and treaty benefit. Conflict: Directive could give 0% but anti-avoidance override may apply if SPV is used to channel payments to a third country. Treaty interest rate: 0% (Art. 11(2) PL-IE DTC with MLI modifications). Expected: UNCERTAIN — legal conflict between Directive path and BO test outcome.

input.substance_notes: Irish special purpose vehicle. Dublin registered address. One non-resident director. No employees, no local management. Issued bonds to raise funds; on-lends to Polish subsidiary. Interest received from Poland is used to service bonds held by non-EU investors. Thin capitalisation: 99% debt-financed. No independent treasury risk management in Ireland.

expected.eval_note: 0% (Directive or treaty) and 10% (domestic fallback if treaty denied) are both acceptable for the rate check. UNCERTAIN on BO is the key assertion.
